This page brings together practical information to help you plan your visit to Glasgow for the EnviBayes-INLA workshop. Sections marked coming soon will be updated as details are confirmed — check back closer to the event.

🏫 Venue Information

The conference will take place on the Gilmorehill Campus of the University of Glasgow.

Workshop tutorials

School of Maths & Stats

  • Pre-workshop tutorials

🎤 Talks & Postets

Sir Charles Wilson Building

  • Registration
  • Keynote panel sessions
  • Contributed talks & poster sessions

🍽️ Food

JMS Learning Hub & Gilchrist Postgrad Club

Food Voucher can be redeemed for

  • Breakfast
  • Lunch
  • Coffee and refreshments

🏨 Accommodation

The University of Glasgow is situated in the West End, with a variety of small hotels and guest houses nearby, as well as easy links to City Centre hotels via the Subway.

The closest Subway station to the main University campus is Hillhead. For City Centre hotels, the closest stations are Buchanan Street and St. Enoch. The journey between the West End and City Centre takes around 10 minutes by Subway.

Delegates are advised to book early to guarantee availability at their preferred hotel.

West End hotels (walking distance to campus)

Glasgow Grosvenor Hotel

✈️ Getting to Glasgow

Travel information coming soon. This section will include guidance on arriving by air (Glasgow International and Edinburgh airports), rail connections to Glasgow Central and Queen Street stations, and intercity coach services. Check back closer to the event.

🚇 Getting Around Glasgow

Local transport guidance coming soon. This section will cover the Glasgow Subway (SPT), local bus services, cycling, and walking routes between key conference locations. A map of the campus and nearby amenities will also be included.

♿ Accessibility

Accessibility information coming soon. This section will include details on step-free access to conference venues, quiet spaces, dietary accommodation, and how to contact the organisers with specific requirements. We are committed to making this event as accessible as possible.

🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 Exploring Glasgow

Visitor recommendations coming soon. Glasgow has a thriving arts, food, and music scene. This section will feature organiser-curated suggestions for restaurants, museums, day trips, and excursions to make the most of your time in the city.
